entries.definition:
كتاب الواو

ولي

الصّحارى. ومنه التَّوليه: أن يفرَّق بين المرأة وولدِها. وفي الحديث: "لا تولّه والدةٌ عن وَلَدها".

(ولي) الواو واللام والياء: أصلٌ صحيح يدلُّ على قرب. من ذلك الوَلْيُ: القرْب. يقال: تَباعَدَ بعد وَلْي، أي قُرْبٍ. وجَلَسَ ممّا يَلِيني، أي يُقارِبُني. والوَلِيُّ: المَطَر يجيءُ بعد الوَسْميّ، سمِّي بذلك لأنَّه يلي الوسمِيّ.

ومن الباب المَوْلَى: المُعْتِقُ والمُعْتَق، والصَّاحب، والحليف، وابن العَمّ، والنَّاصر، والجار؛ كلُّ هؤلاءِ من الوَلْيِ وهو القُرْب. وكلُّ مَن ولِيَ أمرَ آخرَ فهو وليُّه. وفلانٌ أولى بكذا، [أي أحرى به وأجدر. فأمَّا قولهم في الشتم: أولى لكَ فحدّثني علي بن عمر قال: سمعت ثعلباً ] يقول: أولى تهدُّد ووعيد. وأنشد:

وهل للدَّرِّ يُحْلَبُ مِن مَرَدِّ

فأوْلَى ثمَّ أولَى ثم أوْلَى

وقال الأصمعيّ: معناه قارَبَه ما يُهلكُه، أي نَزَل به. وأنشد:

وأولَى أن يزيدَ على الثَّلاثِ

فعَادَى بين هادِيَتينِ منها

أي قارب أن يزيد. قال ثعلب: ولم يقل أحدٌ [أحسَنَ ] مما قاله الأصمعيُّ في أولى. وقال غيره: أولى تحسيرٌ له على ما فاتَه. والوَلاَء: الموالون. يقال هَؤلاء وَلاءُ فلانٍ. والوَلاء أيضاً: ولاءُ المُعْتَق، وهو أن يكون ولاؤه لمُعْتِقِه، كأنَّه يكون أولى به في الإرْثِ من غيره إذا لم يكن للمُعْتِق وارثُ نَسَب. وهو الذي جاء في

ولب - ولث - ولج

الحديث: "نَهَى عن بيع الوَلاَء وهِبَتِه". ووالَيْتُ بين الشَّيئين، إذا عادَيْتَ بينهما وِلاءً. وافعَلْ هذا على الوِلاء أي مُرَتِّبا . والباب كلُّه راجعٌ إلى القُرْب.

و/ل/ي w–l–y adjacency; to follow; to turn to/from; to befriend, to
back up; to take control; helper, backer, guardian, benefactor;
next of kin; patron, servant/master; companion, partner. As
adjacency is the central meaning of this root some derivatives
denote opposite meanings, e.g. master/servant. Of this root, 12
forms occur 233 times in the Qur’an: يَلِي yali once; وَلَّى wallā 30
times; تَوَلَّى tawallā 79 times; وَالٍ wālin once; مُوَلِّي muwallī once;
وَلِيّ waliyy 44 times; أَوْلِيَاء ʾawliyāʾ 42 times; وَلَايَة walāyatun twice;
أَوْلَى ʾawlā 11 times; أَوْلَيَان ʾawlayān once; مَوْلَى mawlā 18 times and
مَوَالِي mawālī three times.

يَلِي yali [imperf. of v. وَلِي waliya, trans.] to be adjacent to, to be
alongside, to be next in place, time or relationship (9:123) قَاتِلُوا الَّذِينَ
يَلُونَكُمْ مِنَ الْكُفَّارِ fight those of the disbelievers who are next to you.

وَلَّى wallā [v. II] I [intrans.] 1 to turn away, to recoil (31:7) وَإِذَا
تُتْلَى عَلَيْهِ آيَاتُنَا وَلَّى مُسْتَكْبِرًا when Our verses are recited to him, he
turns away disdainfully 2 to turn, to face (2:115) فَأَيْنَمَا تُوَلُّوا فَثَمَّ وَجْهُ
اللَّهِ wherever you turn, there is God’s face II [trans.] 1 to turn
something (2:177) لَيْسَ الْبِرَّ أَنْ تُوَلُّوا وُجُوهَكُمْ قِبَلَ الْمَشْرِقِ وَالْمَغْرِبِ
goodness does not [merely] consist in turning your face towards
East or West [in any particular direction] 2 [with prep. عَنْ] to
make turn away (2:142) مَا وَلَّاهُمْ عَنْ قِبْلَتِهِمُ الَّتِي كَانُوا عَلَيْهَا what has
made them turn away from the prayer direction they used to face?
*(48:22) لَوَلَّوُا الْأَدْبَارَ they would have taken flight [lit. turned their
backs/tails] III [doubly trans.] 1 to cause something to turn away
from (8:15) فَلَا تُوَلُّوهُمُ الْأَدْبَارَ never turn your backs on them 2 to give
authority over, to cause to be inclined towards (6:129) وَكَذَلِكَ نُوَلِّي
بَعْضَ الظَّالِمِينَ بَعْضًا in this way, We give some of the unjust power
over others; *(4:115) نُوَلِّهِ مَا تَوَلَّى We will leave him to his own
devices [lit. turn him towards his chosen path].

تَوَلَّى tawallā [v. V] I [intrans.] 1 to leave, to go one’s own way
(2:205) وَإِذَا تَوَلَّى سَعَى فِي الْأَرْضِ لِيُفْسِدَ فِيهَا and when he goes away
[from you] he sets out in the land to spread corruption therein 2
to turn away, to run away (3:155) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ تَوَلَّوْا مِنْكُمْ يَوْمَ الْتَقَى الْجَمْعَانِ
those of you who turned away on the day the two armies met [in
battle] 3 to wander off, to withdraw (28:24) فَسَقَى لَهُمَا ثُمَّ تَوَلَّى إِلَى الظِّلِّ
he watered [their flock] for them, then withdrew into the shade

1048
entries.definition_english:

he watered [their flock] for them, then withdrew into the shade

1048

entries.definition:
*(51:39) فَتَوَلَّى بِرُكْنِهِ [Pharaoh] drew power from his position, he
turned away [also interpreted as: he drew power from his
supporters] II [trans.] 1 to take charge of, to take upon oneself
(24:11) وَالَّذِي تَوَلَّى كِبْرَهُ مِنْهُمْ لَهُ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ he who took upon himself
the greatest part in it will have a painful chastisement 2 to ally
oneself to, to seek support from, to give support to (5:56) وَمَنْ يَتَوَلَّ
اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا فَإِنَّ حِزْبَ اللَّهِ هُمُ الْغَالِبُونَ whoever allies
themselves to God, His Messenger and those who believe—the
party of God surely will be triumphant.

وَالٍ wālin [act. part.] protector, supporter, guardian, ally (13:11)
وَمَا لَهُمْ مِنْ دُونِهِ مِنْ وَالٍ apart from Him, they have no protector.

مُوَلِّي muwallī [act. part.] one facing or turning to or towards
something, one causing someone to face or turn towards
something (2:148) وَلِكُلٍّ وِجْهَةٌ هُوَ مُوَلِّيهَا for each one there is a
direction he takes (or, according to another interpretation: He
[God] directs him towards it).

وَلِيّ waliyy [quasi-act. part./n., pl. أَوْلِيَاء ʾawliyāʾ] 1 protector,
patron, ally (6:14) أَغَيْرَ اللَّهِ أَتَّخِذُ وَلِيًّا فَاطِرِ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ shall I take
for myself a protector other than God, the Creator of the heavens
and the earth? 2 follower, ward, protégé (10:62) أَلَا إِنَّ أَوْلِيَاءَ اللَّهِ لَا
خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ as for those who are close to God [lit.
follower, or, ward of God], there [should] be no concern about
them, nor shall they grieve 3 ally, friend (3:28) لَا يَتَّخِذِ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ
الْكَافِرِينَ أَوْلِيَاءَ مِنْ دُونِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ the believers should not take the
disbelievers as friends/allies in preference to believers 4 [jur.]
legal guardian (2:282) فَإِنْ كَانَ الَّذِي عَلَيْهِ الْحَقُّ سَفِيهًا أَوْ ضَعِيفًا أَوْ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُ أَنْ
يُمِلَّ هُوَ فَلْيُمْلِلْ وَلِيُّهُ بِالْعَدْلِ if the debtor is feeble-minded, weak or
unable to dictate, then let his guardian dictate justly 5 [jur.] next
of kin (17:33) وَمَنْ قُتِلَ مَظْلُومًا فَقَدْ جَعَلْنَا لِوَلِيِّهِ سُلْطَانًا and whoever is slain
unjustly—We have given mandate to the custodian of his rights 6
descendant, heir (19:5–6) فَهَبْ لِي مِنْ لَدُنْكَ وَلِيًّا يَرِثُنِي so grant me a
successor who will inherit me.

مَوْلَى mawlā [quasi-act. part./n., pl. مَوَالِي mawālī] 1 master,
protector, patron (22:78) وَاعْتَصِمُوا بِاللَّهِ هُوَ مَوْلَاكُمْ and hold fast to
God—He is your Protector 2 ally, friend (44:41) يَوْمَ لَا يُغْنِي مَوْلًى عَنْ

مولى شيئًا a Day when no friend can take another’s place 3 kinfolk,
dependants (19:5) وَإِنِّي خِفْتُ الْمَوَالِيَ مِنْ وَرَائِي I fear [what] my kinfolk
[may do] after me 4 [jur.] a freed slave (33:5) فَإِنْ لَمْ تَعْلَمُوا آبَاءَهُمْ
فَإِخْوَانُكُمْ فِي الدِّينِ وَمَوَالِيكُمْ if you do not know their fathers, [address
them as] your brothers-in-religion and mawālī (i.e., wards,
protégés) 5 inheritor, heir (4:33) وَلِكُلٍّ جَعَلْنَا مَوَالِيَ مِمَّا تَرَكَ الْوَالِدَانِ
وَالْأَقْرَبُونَ and for everything that parents and close relatives leave
behind We have appointed heirs.

وَلَايَة walāyatun (also وِلَايَة wilāyatun) [v. n./n.] 1 support,
backing, succour (8:72) وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَلَمْ يُهَاجِرُوا مَا لَكُمْ مِنْ وَلَايَتِهِمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ
and those who believed but did not emigrate, you have
nothing whatsoever to do with their protection until they emigrate
2 sovereignty, sovereign power (18:44) الْوَلَايَةُ لِلَّهِ الْحَقِّ sovereignty
is for God [alone], the True One.

أَوْلَى ʾawlā [elat.] more/most deserving, having more/most right
or claim to (3:68) إِنَّ أَوْلَى النَّاسِ بِإِبْرَاهِيمَ لَلَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُ وَهَذَا النَّبِيُّ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا
the people who are the most deserving of Abraham[’s legacy] are
those who truly follow him, this Prophet, and those who believe;
*(75:34) أَوْلَى لَكَ either from وَلِيَ waliya, to come closely behind,
(elliptically) [your end is] nearer to you, or from وَيْل wayl, agony;
distress, woe to you!

أَوْلَيَان ʾawlayān [dual n.] the two more/most deserving, the two
having more/most priority, the nearer/nearest; closer/closest
(5:107) فَآخَرَانِ يَقُومَانِ مَقَامَهُمَا مِنَ الَّذِينَ اسْتَحَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ الْأَوْلَيَانِ
if it is discovered that these two have committed [lit. a
sin] perjury, two others [should] replace them from among those
of the family of the deceased whose rights have been usurped,
being the ones more deserving of standing witness (or, being the
nearer to the deceased).
entries.definition_english:

فَإِخْوَانُكُمْ فِي الدِّينِ وَمَوَالِيكُمْ if you do not know their fathers, [address
them as] your brothers-in-religion and mawālī (i.e., wards,
protégés) 5 inheritor, heir (4:33) وَلِكُلٍّ جَعَلْنَا مَوَالِيَ مِمَّا تَرَكَ الْوَالِدَانِ
وَالْأَقْرَبُونَ and for everything that parents and close relatives leave
behind We have appointed heirs.

وَلَايَة walāyatun (also وِلَايَة wilāyatun) [v. n./n.] 1 support,
backing, succour (8:72) وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَلَمْ يُهَاجِرُوا مَا لَكُمْ مِنْ وَلَايَتِهِمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ
and those who believed but did not emigrate, you have
nothing whatsoever to do with their protection until they emigrate
2 sovereignty, sovereign power (18:44) الْوَلَايَةُ لِلَّهِ الْحَقِّ sovereignty
is for God [alone], the True One.

أَوْلَى ʾawlā [elat.] more/most deserving, having more/most right
or claim to (3:68) إِنَّ أَوْلَى النَّاسِ بِإِبْرَاهِيمَ لَلَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُ وَهَذَا النَّبِيُّ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا
the people who are the most deserving of Abraham[’s legacy] are
those who truly follow him, this Prophet, and those who believe;
*(75:34) أَوْلَى لَكَ either from وَلِيَ waliya, to come closely behind,
(elliptically) [your end is] nearer to you, or from وَيْل wayl, agony;
distress, woe to you!

أَوْلَيَان ʾawlayān [dual n.] the two more/most deserving, the two
having more/most priority, the nearer/nearest; closer/closest
(5:107) فَآخَرَانِ يَقُومَانِ مَقَامَهُمَا مِنَ الَّذِينَ اسْتَحَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ الْأَوْلَيَانِ
if it is discovered that these two have committed [lit. a
sin] perjury, two others [should] replace them from among those
of the family of the deceased whose rights have been usurped,
being the ones more deserving of standing witness (or, being the
nearer to the deceased).
